Lead.title: Lead
Lead.description: Represents a potential customer who has submitted their initial contact information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lead entity.

name.description: The full name of the lead.

email.description: The email address of the lead.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the lead record was created.

mortgageProfileId.description: Reference to the associated MortgageProfile. (Relationship: Lead 1:1 MortgageProfile)

hasPurchasedMethod.description: A flag indicating whether the lead has purchased the 'Mortgage Cutter Method'.

MortgageProfile.title: MortgageProfile
MortgageProfile.description: Stores detailed mortgage information provided by a lead for personalized savings estimates.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MortgageProfile entity.

leadId.description: Reference to the Lead associated with this mortgage profile. (Relationship: Lead 1:1 MortgageProfile)

currentBalance.description: The current outstanding balance of the mortgage.

interestRate.description: The current annual interest rate of the mortgage (e.g., 3.5 for 3.5%).

originalLoanAmount.description: The original amount of the mortgage loan.

loanTermYears.description: The original term of the loan in years (e.g., 30 for 30 years).

monthlyPayment.description: The principal and interest portion of the monthly mortgage payment.

yearsInHome.description: The number of years the homeowner has been in their home/paying the mortgage.

estimatedHomeValue.description: The estimated current market value of the home. (Optional)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this mortgage profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this mortgage profile was last updated.

SavingsProjection.title: SavingsProjection
SavingsProjection.description: Stores the calculated financial benefits of using the 'Mortgage Cutter Method' compared to traditional payoff.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SavingsProjection entity.

mortgageProfileId.description: Reference to the MortgageProfile for which this projection was made. (Relationship: MortgageProfile 1:1 SavingsProjection)

traditionalTotalInterestPaid.description: The total estimated interest paid under the traditional mortgage payoff schedule.

traditionalYearsToPayOff.description: The total estimated years to pay off the mortgage under the traditional schedule.

cutterTotalInterestPaid.description: The total estimated interest paid using the 'Mortgage Cutter Method'.

cutterYearsToPayOff.description: The total estimated years to pay off the mortgage using the 'Mortgage Cutter Method'.

yearsShavedOff.description: The number of years shaved off the mortgage payoff time with the 'Mortgage Cutter Method'.

moneySaved.description: The total amount of money saved on interest with the 'Mortgage Cutter Method'.

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when this savings projection was generated.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a purchasable item, such as the 'Mortgage Cutter Method'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The name of the product (e.g., 'Mortgage Cutter Method').

description.description: A brief description of the product.

currency.description: The currency of the product's price (e.g., 'USD').

accessContentUrl.description: The URL where the purchased content (e.g., method guide, tools) can be accessed.

Purchase.title: Purchase
Purchase.description: Records a successful transaction where a lead purchases a product.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Purchase entity.

leadId.description: Reference to the Lead who made the purchase. (Relationship: Lead 1:N Purchase)

productId.description: Reference to the Product that was purchased. (Relationship: Product 1:N Purchase)

amount.description: The actual amount paid for the purchase.

currency.description: The currency used for the purchase.

transactionId.description: The unique transaction ID provided by the external payment gateway.

purchaseDate.description: Timestamp when the purchase was completed.

status.description: The current status of the purchase (e.g., 'completed', 'failed', 'refunded').

AccessGrant.title: AccessGrant
AccessGrant.description: Records that a lead has been granted access to a specific product following a successful purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AccessGrant entity.

leadId.description: Reference to the Lead who received access. (Relationship: Lead 1:N AccessGrant)

productId.description: Reference to the Product to which access was granted. (Relationship: Product 1:N AccessGrant)

purchaseId.description: Reference to the Purchase that led to this access grant. (Relationship: Purchase 1:1 AccessGrant)

isRevoked.description: A flag indicating if the access has been revoked (e.g., due to a refund).

definition.description: Represents a potential customer. The document ID for each Lead corresponds to the user's Firebase UID (request.auth.uid). Includes denormalized 'hasPurchasedMethod' flag for authorization independence when checking access to paid content.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique identifier for the lead, which is also the user's Firebase UID.

definition.description: Stores the single, detailed mortgage information for a specific lead. This is a 1:1 relationship with Lead. The document ID is a fixed string 'mortgageProfile'. The document includes 'leadId' (acting as 'ownerId') from the schema for authorization independence.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique identifier for the lead, corresponding to the parent Lead document's ID.

definition.description: Stores the single, calculated financial benefits projection for a specific lead. This is a 1:1 relationship with Lead. The document ID is a fixed string 'savingsProjection'. The document will include 'leadId' as 'ownerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique identifier for the lead, corresponding to the parent Lead document's ID.

definition.description: Publicly accessible catalog of products offered on the site. Contains product details like name, description, and price. The 'accessContentUrl' field in this collection should not be directly used for content access without verifying an AccessGrant.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product.

definition.description: Records individual successful transactions made by a lead. The document includes 'leadId' from the schema (acting as 'ownerId') for authorization independence.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique identifier for the lead who made the purchase, corresponding to the parent Lead document's ID.

params.name: purchaseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific purchase transaction.

definition.description: Records that a specific lead has been granted access to a product. This collection is critical for controlling access to paid content. The document includes 'leadId' from the schema (acting as 'ownerId') for authorization independence.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique identifier for the lead who received the access grant, corresponding to the parent Lead document's ID.

params.name: accessGrantId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific access grant.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to adhere strictly to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence, by leveraging path-based ownership and strategic denormalization. The primary user-centric entity is the 'Lead', and its document ID is directly tied to the authenticated user's Firebase UID (request.auth.uid). This establishes a strong, clear ownership model.

Here’s how the design achieves Authorization Independence and supports QAPs:

1.  **Lead as the User Root ("/leads/{leadId}"):** Each authenticated user (or anonymous user) will have a corresponding 'Lead' document where `leadId` is their `request.auth.uid`. This makes all operations on a user's primary record simple to secure: `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == leadId;`.

2.  **Denormalization for Subcollections (Mandate A):**
    *   For subcollections like `mortgageProfile` (the single document representing the MortgageProfile), `savingsProjection` (the single document representing the SavingsProjection), `purchases`, and `accessGrants`, each document will explicitly include an `ownerId` field (which will store the `leadId`). This is critical. Security rules for these subcollections will verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId` instead of performing a `get()` call to the parent `/leads/{leadId}` document. This ensures that creating, updating, or deleting these sub-documents can be done atomically without needing to read the parent document for authorization, fulfilling the Authorization Independence mandate.

3.  **Denormalization for Content Access (`hasPurchasedMethod`):**
    *   The `Lead` document at `/leads/{leadId}` contains a denormalized `hasPurchasedMethod` boolean flag. This flag is updated via a backend function (e.g., Cloud Function) in a batched write or transaction whenever an `AccessGrant` is successfully created or revoked for the 'Mortgage Cutter Method' product. This is a critical invariant. To determine if a user can access the method content, security rules can perform a single `get()` on the user's *own* `Lead` document (`get(/databases/$(database)/documents/leads/$(request.auth.uid)).data.hasPurchasedMethod == true`). This `get()` is acceptable as it's on the *user's own root document* to check a global state for that user, rather than relying on a hierarchical parent dependency to authorize a *child document*'s direct access.

4.  **Structural Segregation (Mandate B) and QAPs (Mandate D):**
    *   **"/products/{productId}":** This collection is designed for homogeneous security (public read-only) to serve as a product catalog. This separation allows clients to query and display product information (e.g., name, price, description) without granting access to sensitive content URLs directly from the product document itself. This supports QAPs because listing products is an unrestricted read.
    *   All user-specific data (Lead, MortgageProfile, SavingsProjection, Purchases, AccessGrants) is nested under the "/leads/{leadId}" path, ensuring that `list` operations on these collections (e.g., `db.collection('leads').doc(uid).collection('purchases').get()`) are implicitly scoped to the authenticated user, satisfying QAP by returning only data the user is authorized to see.

5.  **Access Modeling (Mandate C):**
    *   **Private Data:** All personal data (`Lead`, `MortgageProfile`, `SavingsProjection`, `Purchase`, `AccessGrant`) is secured using path-based ownership under "/leads/{leadId}/..." where `{leadId}` maps to `request.auth.uid`.
    *   **Hierarchical Paths:** The 1:N relationships (Lead to Purchases, Lead to AccessGrants) and 1:1 relationships (`Lead` to `MortgageProfile`, `Lead` to `SavingsProjection`) are modeled using subcollections for clear ownership and organization.

This structure ensures clear authorization intent, simplifies security rules, and allows for efficient and secure data access patterns.
